issues.description: Directions could specify minimum response length or structure requirements
Prompts could benefit from more explicit connection to the two-part standard (explicit text analysis + inference)
Prompt 3 asking to distinguish between inform/persuade/entertain may be slightly advanced for some 6th graders

strengths: Excellent alignment with RI.6.1 standard requiring both textual evidence citation and inference skills
Strong exemplar demonstrates proper format with quotes, page numbers, and clear reasoning
Prompts progress logically from basic inference to more complex analysis
Age-appropriate topics (climate change, social media, space exploration) that engage 6th grade interests
Clear focus on supporting claims with evidence throughout all activities
Neutral worldview maintained across all content
Good variety in prompt formats while maintaining consistent skill focus

detailed_feedback.structure: Well-organized with complete template compliance and logical progression of difficulty
detailed_feedback.alignment: Strong alignment with RI.6.1 standard, effectively targeting both explicit analysis and inference skills
detailed_feedback.clarity: Generally clear instructions, though could benefit from more specific formatting requirements
detailed_feedback.difficulty: Appropriately challenging for grade 6 with good scaffolding through exemplar
detailed_feedback.inclusivity: Topics are broadly accessible and culturally neutral
detailed_feedback.accuracy: Content is factually sound and pedagogically appropriate for the target standard
